reforms.description: The government has implemented a series of measures aimed at enhancing the startup ecosystem, including a reduction of the corporation tax rate for SMEs to 12½ percent to attract venture capital and support new enterprises. Additionally, a proposed framework for greenhouse gas taxation is set to support energy-efficiency measures, R&D, and information programs, with the revenue aimed at reducing direct taxes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These measures are designed to stimulate entrepreneurship and innovation, which are critical for economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The measures have been put into effect as part of the government's strategy to enhance the startup ecosystem.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reduction in corporate tax rates and the introduction of a greenhouse gas taxation framework represent significant structural changes to the innovation landscape.
reforms.source_quote: The reduction of the corporation tax rate for SMEs in this year's budget to 12½ per cent should encourage the development of new enterprises and further inflows of venture capital.

reforms.description: The government has established several initiatives to enhance human capital, including a skills fund to support enterprise training, a skills-based work permit system for non-EU nationals, and the National Training Fund to finance in-company training. Additionally, a Training Networks Programme was created to improve companies' capacity to address shared training needs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These initiatives aim to address skill shortages and enhance workforce capabilities, which are essential for economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: These programs have been actively implemented to improve skills and training in the workforce.
reforms.importance_rationale: While these initiatives are significant, they are not as transformative as major reforms.
reforms.source_quote: A skills fund has been set up to support enterprise training but with the labour market tight, the logic of the programme, which still needs to be more closely specified, is not clear.

reforms.description: The National Development Plan has been initiated to address infrastructure deficits and enhance education, with a significant allocation of funds for R&D and support for indigenous industries. However, it has been noted that the plan lacks a focus on environmental economics and policy analysis.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The funding for R&D and support for indigenous industries are expected to enhance innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan has been put into effect with allocations for R&D and infrastructure improvements.
reforms.importance_rationale: The National Development Plan represents a major commitment to funding R&D and improving infrastructure.
reforms.source_quote: The productive sector programme envisages Ir£ 4.5 billion for a major extension of spending on R&D as well as a continuation of existing support schemes for indigenous industries.

reforms.description: A proposed framework for greenhouse gas taxation aims to support energy-efficiency measures, R&D, and information programs, with the revenue used to reduce direct taxes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The framework is designed to promote energy efficiency and support R&D, which can lead to innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The framework has been proposed and is awaiting implementation.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, the framework is not as transformative as major reforms.
reforms.source_quote: A key element of the Strategy is the proposal to have a framework for greenhouse gas taxation in place from 2002.
